ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Coca-Cola Less Sugar; FOODON_03301589 (carbonated soft drink) is the closest defensible class-level match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers cola-type soft drinks; no specific reduced-sugar cola code exists in the published FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for a specific commercial reduced-sugar cola product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of Coca-Cola Less Sugar is 330 ml, equivalent to approximately 330 g, and is the most common retail portion consumed in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Coca-Cola Less Sugar — Nutrition Information Panel (Singapore product label) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: Coca-Cola Singapore (product label) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The input record's per_100g values (412 kcal, 12.5 g fat, 5.55 g protein, 69.4 g carbohydrate, 20.8 g sugar) are clearly inconsistent with any carbonated cola beverage and appear to be a data-entry error or misassignment. Actual Coca-Cola Less Sugar (Singapore label) reports approximately: energy 5 kcal/100 ml, carbohydrate 1.1 g/100 ml, sugar 1.1 g/100 ml, protein 0 g, fat 0 g, sodium 10 mg/100 ml. USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174851 (carbonated beverage, cola, with caffeine) reports: energy 37 kcal/100 g, carbohydrate 9.56 g, sugar 9.56 g, protein 0.07 g, fat 0 g, sodium 4 mg. For a reduced-sugar cola, the Singapore product label values are used as the primary source, with USDA FDC 174851 as a secondary cross-reference for nutrient completeness. The input per_100g values have been replaced with product-label-consistent values. This substitution is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of artificially sweetened cola beverages — analogous estimate based on Diet Coke / reduced-sugar cola data from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Coca-Cola Less Sugar in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 34 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for reduced-sugar and artificially sweetened cola beverages (Diet Coke GI ~0–34, Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr). Given the very low carbohydrate content (1.1 g/100 ml), the glycemic load per 330 ml serving is negligible: GL = (34 × 1.1 × 3.3) / 100 = 1.2 / 100 × 330 ≈ 0.4. This is classified as low GI and negligible GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Caffeine content and SSB classification; sodium is low (~10 mg/100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola Less Sugar contains caffeine (~34 mg/330 ml can), which causes acute transient blood pressure elevation; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to occasional use and prefer caffeine-free alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Artificial sweeteners (aspartame, acesulfame K) and SSB classification despite low sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although sugar content is low (~1.1 g/100 ml), Coca-Cola Less Sugar contains aspartame and acesulfame potassium, which may maintain sweet-taste preference and have uncertain effects on insulin response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional beverage and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed SSB classification; caramel colour (Class IV) and phosphoric acid; caffeine
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of artificially sweetened cola beverages has been associated with increased cardiovascular risk in observational studies (Malik et al., Circulation 2019); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and prioritise plain water, unsweetened tea, or low-sodium sparkling water.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola Less Sugar is a commercially manufactured, carbonated cola soft drink produced by The Coca-Cola Company and sold in Singapore as a reduced-sugar alternative to regular Coca-Cola. It is an ultra-processed beverage sweetened primarily with artificial sweeteners — aspartame and acesulfame potassium — rather than sucrose, resulting in a very low sugar content of approximately 1.1 g per 100 ml and an energy content of approximately 5 kcal per 100 ml. The beverage has the characteristic crisp, slightly sweet, caramel-cola flavour of Coca-Cola, with carbonation providing effervescence and a refreshing mouthfeel. It contains caffeine (~34 mg per 330 ml can), caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, and natural flavours. Nutritionally, it provides negligible macronutrients: protein is naturally zero (no protein-containing ingredients), fat is naturally zero by product formulation, and carbohydrate is minimal at 1.1 g/100 ml. Sodium is low at approximately 10 mg/100 ml. Despite its low sugar and calorie profile, it is classified as an ultra-processed sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) substitute and carries moderate clinical concern for individuals with hypertension (due to caffeine), Type 2 Diabetes (due to artificial sweeteners and SSB classification), and cardiovascular disease (due to regular SSB consumption patterns). It is widely available in Singapore in 330 ml cans and 1.5 L bottles. The product carries a PKU warning as it contains phenylalanine from aspartame.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from the Coca-Cola Less Sugar Singapore product nutrition information panel (primary) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174851 (carbonated cola beverage). The input record's per_100g values were identified as a clear data-entry error (values consistent with a solid food, not a beverage) and replaced with product-label-consistent values. The substitution is fully documented.
notes: CRITICAL DATA CORRECTION: The input record's per_100g values (energy 412 kcal, protein 5.55 g, fat 12.5 g, carbohydrate 69.4 g, sugar 20.8 g, sodium 312 mg, dietary fibre 3.1 g, potassium 199 mg, calcium 417 mg, magnesium 48 mg, iron 2.03 mg) are entirely inconsistent with Coca-Cola Less Sugar, which is a carbonated beverage. These values appear to be a misassignment from a solid food record. All per_100g values have been replaced with product-label-consistent values sourced from the Coca-Cola Less Sugar Singapore nutrition information panel. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: protein_g is naturally zero — this is a carbonated water-based beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — confirmed zero per product label and USDA FoodData Central FDC 174851. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all naturally zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero — carbonated beverages contain no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero — no animal-derived ingredients. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported on the Singapore product nutrition information panel and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific reduced-sugar cola formulation. GI NOTE: GI of 34 is an analogous estimate based on Diet Coke / reduced-sugar cola data from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI entry exists for Coca-Cola Less Sugar. GL per 330 ml serving = 0.4 (negligible). PKU WARNING: Contains phenylalanine (from aspartame). HALAL STATUS: Coca-Cola Less Sugar is certified halal in Singapore by MUIS.
